orchagent.purpose: Central orchestration agent. Subscribes to APPL_DB tables (routes, neighbors, ports, VLANs, ACLs, tunnels) and translates each desired-state entry into SAI API calls written to ASIC_DB. Syncd consumes ASIC_DB to drive the vendor SDK. Orchagent is the single point of ASIC programming for all SONiC features.

syncd.purpose: SAI-to-SDK bridge. Consumes ASIC_DB entries written by orchagent and calls the vendor SAI implementation (libsai.so) to program the forwarding ASIC. Also polls ASIC counters via the flex-counter framework and publishes them to COUNTERS_DB. Vendor-specific; each platform ships its own libsai.

fpmsyncd.purpose: FPM (Forwarding Plane Manager) synchronization daemon. Receives RIB updates from FRR's zebra process via the FPM socket (Unix socket, default port 2620) and writes them as ROUTE_TABLE entries into APPL_DB. Bridges the FRR routing stack and the SONiC forwarding pipeline.

bgpcfgd.purpose: BGP configuration daemon. Watches CONFIG_DB for BGP neighbor, peer-group, prefix-list, route-map, and global ASN changes and translates them into FRR vtysh configuration commands applied live. Ensures FRR configuration stays in sync with CONFIG_DB without requiring a BGP container restart.

portsyncd.purpose: Port synchronization daemon. Reads port configuration (speed, MTU, admin state, FEC mode, auto-negotiation) from CONFIG_DB and writes desired state to APPL_DB:PORT_TABLE. Also monitors netlink events for kernel-level link state changes and publishes them to STATE_DB:PORT_TABLE, gating other daemons that wait for port readiness.

neighsyncd.purpose: Neighbor synchronization daemon. Monitors the kernel ARP/NDP table via netlink NEIGH events and writes resolved neighbor entries (IP-to-MAC mappings) to APPL_DB:NEIGH_TABLE. Orchagent converts these into SAI neighbor and next-hop objects in ASIC_DB.

portmgrd.purpose: Port manager daemon. Subscribes to CONFIG_DB:PORT table and applies port configuration to the Linux kernel (via netlink/ip commands): sets MTU, admin state (ip link set up/down), and interface description. Counterpart to portsyncd; portmgrd handles the kernel side while portsyncd handles the SAI/APPL_DB side.

intfmgrd.purpose: Interface manager daemon. Subscribes to CONFIG_DB:INTERFACE and CONFIG_DB:VLAN_INTERFACE tables and applies IP address assignments to Linux kernel interfaces. Writes interface entries to APPL_DB for orchagent to create the corresponding RIF (Router Interface) SAI objects. Handles IPv4 and IPv6 address add/delete events.

vlanmgrd.purpose: VLAN manager daemon. Subscribes to CONFIG_DB:VLAN and CONFIG_DB:VLAN_MEMBER tables and creates/destroys Linux VLAN bridge interfaces and bridge FDB entries. Writes VLAN and VLAN member entries to APPL_DB so orchagent can create the corresponding SAI VLAN objects and port membership.

teammgrd.purpose: Team (LAG/PortChannel) manager daemon. Subscribes to CONFIG_DB:PORTCHANNEL and CONFIG_DB:PORTCHANNEL_MEMBER tables and manages the teamd (libteam) process lifecycle: starts/stops a teamd instance per PortChannel and adds/removes member ports. Writes LAG state to APPL_DB:LAG_TABLE for orchagent.

teamsyncd.purpose: Team synchronization daemon. Subscribes to LAG state changes from the teamd processes (via libteamdctl) and publishes LAG member port state (selected/deselected by LACP) to APPL_DB:LAG_MEMBER_TABLE. Orchagent uses this to add or remove ports from the SAI LAG object.

lldpsyncd.purpose: LLDP synchronization daemon. Polls the lldpd process for neighbor information (via lldpcli) and publishes discovered LLDP neighbor entries to APPL_DB:LLDP_ENTRY_TABLE. These entries are used by the topology discovery CLI and optionally by lldpmgrd for interface description auto-population.

lldpmgrd.purpose: LLDP manager daemon. Subscribes to CONFIG_DB:DEVICE_METADATA and CONFIG_DB:PORT tables and configures the lldpd process accordingly: sets the chassis ID, system description, and per-port LLDP parameters (admin status, tx/rx enable). Translates SONiC CONFIG_DB intent into lldpcli commands.

xcvrd.purpose: Transceiver daemon. Manages pluggable optics and DAC cables: reads EEPROM data (vendor, part number, serial, DOM measurements) from SFP/QSFP/OSFP modules via the platform SFP API and publishes it to STATE_DB:TRANSCEIVER_INFO and STATE_DB:TRANSCEIVER_DOM_SENSOR tables. Also handles module initialization sequences and high-power module power-up state machines.

nbrmgrd.purpose: Neighbor manager daemon. Manages static ARP/NDP entries and neighbor probing from CONFIG_DB. Writes neighbor entries to kernel via netlink.

buffermgrd.purpose: Buffer manager daemon. Translates CONFIG_DB buffer pool/profile/PG/queue configuration into APPL_DB entries for orchagent to program SAI buffer objects.

coppmgrd.purpose: CoPP (Control Plane Policing) manager daemon. Reads COPP_TABLE from CONFIG_DB and writes CoPP trap/policer entries to APPL_DB for orchagent to program in SAI.

tunnelmgrd.purpose: Tunnel manager daemon. Manages VXLAN tunnel configuration from CONFIG_DB and writes tunnel entries to APPL_DB for orchagent VxlanTunnelOrch.

sflowmgrd.purpose: sFlow manager daemon. Manages sFlow sampling configuration from CONFIG_DB.

natmgrd.purpose: NAT manager daemon. Manages NAT/NAPT configuration from CONFIG_DB.

macsecmgrd.purpose: MACsec manager daemon. Manages MACsec encryption configuration from CONFIG_DB.

routesyncd.purpose: Route synchronization daemon. Monitors kernel routing table via netlink for directly-connected and locally-originated routes, writes them to APPL_DB:ROUTE_TABLE.

bgpd.purpose: FRR BGP daemon. Implements BGP-4 protocol: establishes sessions, exchanges routes, runs best-path selection. Managed by bgpcfgd; do not configure directly via vtysh.

zebra.purpose: FRR Zebra daemon. Central RIB manager for FRR. Receives routes from all FRR protocol daemons, selects best route, programs kernel FIB via netlink, and notifies fpmsyncd via FPM socket.

staticd.purpose: FRR static route daemon. Manages static routes configured via FRR and installs them into zebra RIB.

frrcfgd.purpose: Unified FRR configuration daemon (alternative to bgpcfgd). Event-driven CONFIG_DB subscriber that supports OSPF, VRFs, IGMP, PIM in addition to BGP. Enabled by setting frr_mgmt_framework_config=true in DEVICE_METADATA.

ospfd.purpose: FRR OSPF daemon. Implements OSPFv2 protocol.

ospf6d.purpose: FRR OSPFv3 daemon. Implements OSPFv3 for IPv6.

isisd.purpose: FRR IS-IS daemon. Implements IS-IS protocol.

bfdd.purpose: FRR BFD daemon. Implements Bidirectional Forwarding Detection for sub-second failure detection.
